sections.paragraph:
μῶμος, ου, ὁ(s. prec. entry; on the etym. s. LfgrE s.v.)

① blame (Hom. et al.; Kaibel 948, 8; Sir 18:15; SibOr 3, 377) δίχα παντὸς μ. without any blame 1 Cl 63:1.

② defect, blemish bodily (Lev 21:17f, 21; 24:19f; TestLevi 9:10) and also moral (Sir 11:31, 33; 20:24; Philo, Sobr. 11 μ. ἐν ψυχῇ) of dissident teachers σπίλοι καὶ μῶμοι blots and blemishes 2 Pt 2:13.—DELG. M-M. TW.

prec. prec. = preceding

etym. etym. = etymology, -ical

LfgrE LfgrE = Lexicon des frühgriechischen Epos—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Kaibel Kaibel = Epigrammata Graeca ex Lapidibus Conlecta, ed. GK.—List 3

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 663
